title: To My Ill Reader.
content:
Thou say'st my lines are hard,
    And I the truth will tell -
    They are both hard and marr'd
    If thou not read'st them well.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Herrick, titled "To My Ill Reader.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
